response_raw:
## 1. Rider objective & context

The footage appears to be a point-of-view (POV) helmet/bike-mounted camera recording of off-road motorcycle riding through a pine forest. At least one additional rider is visible ahead in several frames. The clip was recorded on a DJI Osmo Action 5 Pro, watermarked "2026."

---

## 2. Rider

- **Frames 1, 5, 6:** The camera-carrying rider's red motorcycle (handlebars, front fairing) is visible at the bottom of frame. The rider themselves are not visible — camera appears helmet- or bar-mounted.
- **Frame 3:** A second rider is visible on the right edge, wearing a dark jacket, helmet, and boots, standing beside or astride a red motorcycle (partial "GASGAS" or similar branding visible on shroud). Rider appears stationary.
- **Frame 7 (hilltop):** Two figures with a red motorcycle are visible at the crest of the hill, at distance.
- **Final two frames:** The motorcycle is lying on its side on the ground, unoccupied. The rider is not visible in these frames.

---

## 3. Terrain & environment

- Dense pine forest with tall, closely-spaced trunks.
- Sloped hillside terrain, visibly inclined throughout.
- Ground surface: dry dirt, dead leaves, pine needles, scattered fallen branches and small logs, some moss-covered rocks.
- Tree stumps and cut/broken branches visible throughout.
- No defined trail visible in most frames; faint track lines appear in frames 5–6.
- Overcast/bright sky visible through tree canopy.
- The slope becomes steeper and more debris-strewn in later frames (frames 8–13).
- A prominent leaning/fallen tree trunk visible in frames 9–11.
- Large moss-covered boulders visible on the slope in frames 9–12.

---

## 4. Events

- **Frames 1–2:** POV rider is moving through flat-to-gently-sloped forest; a second rider on a red motorcycle is visible ahead in the distance.
- **Frame 3:** The camera briefly shows the second rider stationary at right side of frame, up close.
- **Frame 4:** Camera view drops toward the ground; the rider and bike are no longer in frame.
- **Frames 5–7:** POV resumes, showing an uphill slope ahead; two riders/bikes visible near the crest.
- **Frames 8–13:** The camera view shows a steep, debris-covered slope. The camera appears to be moving down or across the slope. Bike handlebars and front end are partially visible in frames 9–10, then disappear.
- **Frame 14:** Camera angle is dramatically tilted; the view shows primarily the steep downhill slope with no bike visible.
- **Frame 15:** The red motorcycle is on its side on the ground, resting against the terrain. The exhaust pipe, front wheel, handlebars, and red bodywork are clearly visible. The bike is stationary and on its side.
- **Frame 16:** Closer view of the downed motorcycle on its side; front wheel pointing upward, bodywork (red panels with a square logo visible) in contact with the ground.

---

## 5. Outcome

The motorcycle is shown lying on its side on the forest floor in the final two frames. The bike is visibly on the ground, unoccupied. No rider is visible in these frames.

---

## 6. Unclear or not visible

- The rider's body position and location are not visible in the final frames — it is unclear where the rider is after the motorcycle is on the ground.
- The exact moment of the bike going down is not captured in a single clear frame; it occurs between frames 13 and 15.
- Whether any impact with a tree or obstacle occurred is not clearly visible.
- The rider's physical condition is not visible at any point in the sequence.
- The specific motorcycle brand on the camera bike is not fully legible (partially visible as red bodywork only).
- Speed at any point is not determinable from the frames alone.
- Whether the second rider (frame 3) is the same individual seen at the hilltop is unclear.
